text: Last summer, I finally planned a trip to Japan after saving money for months. It was my first time experiencing an entirely new culture, from bowing instead of handshakes to removing shoes before entering homes. The food was incredible—I tried sushi at a tiny market in Tokyo and spicy ramen in a backstreet shop. However, language barriers were tricky; I often used hand gestures and translation apps to communicate. Despite these challenges, the wandering through ancient temples and chatting with locals created unforgettable memories. Now, I smile every time I see photos of that journey, realizing that stepping out of your comfort zone can bring the most rewarding experiences.
